identity.name: Report
identity.definition: A Report is a system-generated analytics artifact that aggregates student performance data, participation metrics, and per-activity breakdowns from a completed Nearpod lesson delivery.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Report appears in dashboards and recent reports lists

attributes.name: Report Type
attributes.description: Type of delivery: live-session-report, self-paced-report, combined-report.

attributes.name: Participation Rate
attributes.description: Percentage of enrolled students who participated.

attributes.name: Average Score
attributes.description: Mean score across all students and activities.

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How a Report appears in report tables and history views

representations.heading: Detail page layout
representations.description: Full report view with activity breakdowns and student details

attributes.name: Teacher
attributes.description: The teacher who conducted the delivery.

attributes.name: Student Count
attributes.description: Number of students included in the report.

attributes.name: Participation Rate
attributes.description: Percentage of enrolled students who participated.

attributes.name: Average Score
attributes.description: Mean score across all students and scored activities.

attributes.name: Activity Breakdown
attributes.description: Per-activity results including response counts, averages, and distributions.

attributes.name: Time Period
attributes.description: Date range covered by the report.

stories.title: Review Session Results
stories.benefit: I can understand how my class performed and identify students who need additional support.
stories.whenClause: I open a report after a live session ends,
stories.thenClause: I see aggregate participation rates, average scores, per-activity breakdowns, and individual student results.

stories.title: Export Report Data
stories.benefit: I can share results with parents, administrators, or use them for grade book entry.
stories.thenClause: a PDF or CSV file is generated containing all student scores, participation data, and activity breakdowns.

stories.title: Compare Lesson Performance
stories.benefit: I can see how different classes or deliveries of the same lesson compare.
stories.whenClause: I select multiple reports for the same lesson,
stories.thenClause: I see side-by-side comparisons of participation, scores, and per-activity performance across classes.

businessRules.title: Auto-Generation
businessRules.description: Reports are automatically generated by the system when a Live Session ends or a self-paced assignment's due date passes. Teachers cannot manually create reports.

businessRules.title: Processing State
businessRules.description: Reports enter 'processing' state while aggregation is computed. Large classes may take up to 2 minutes. Reports transition to 'available' when complete.

businessRules.title: Data Immutability
businessRules.description: Once generated, report data is immutable. Late submissions on self-paced assignments may trigger a report regeneration.

businessRules.title: Participation Calculation
businessRules.description: Participation Rate is (students who submitted at least one response / total enrolled students) * 100.

businessRules.title: Score Aggregation
businessRules.description: Average Score is calculated across all scored activities only. Unscored activities (polls, open-ended with scoring=none) are excluded from the average.

businessRules.title: Activity Breakdown
businessRules.description: Each scored activity in the report includes: activity title, type, response count, average score, and correct/incorrect distribution.

businessRules.title: Student Detail Access
businessRules.description: Teachers can drill down into individual student responses within a report. Each student's per-activity responses are viewable.

businessRules.title: Export Formats
businessRules.description: Reports can be exported as PDF (formatted summary) or CSV (raw data for spreadsheet analysis). Export includes all student-level detail.

businessRules.title: Archival
businessRules.description: Archived reports are hidden from the default report list but remain accessible via filters. Data is never deleted.

businessRules.title: Combined Reports
businessRules.description: A combined report merges live session and self-paced delivery data for the same lesson and class into a single view.

businessRules.title: Report Retention
businessRules.description: Reports are retained indefinitely. They persist even if the source Lesson, Session, or Assignment is deleted or archived.

states.name: Processing
states.description: Report is being generated and data is being aggregated
states.triggers: Session ends or assignment due date passes

states.name: Available
states.description: Report is complete and viewable by the teacher

states.name: Archived
states.description: Report is hidden from default views but data retained

relationships.mechanics: A Report is generated for a specific Lesson delivery. It references the source Lesson for context (title, activities, standards).
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A Lesson can have many Reports (one per delivery); each Report references exactly one Lesson.
relationships.sorts: By generated date (most recent first), by class.
relationships.filters: By lesson title, by date range, by report type.
relationships.dependencies: The Lesson reference provides context. If the Lesson is deleted, the Report retains a snapshot of the lesson title and activity list.

relationships.mechanics: Reports contain per-student performance data. Teachers can drill into individual student responses.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Report contains data for many Students; a Student appears in many Reports over time.
relationships.sorts: By student name (default), by score (highest/lowest), by participation.
relationships.filters: By score range, by completion status, by individual student.
relationships.dependencies: Student data in the report is a snapshot. If a student transfers or is deactivated, their historical report data persists.

relationships.mechanics: Reports include per-activity breakdowns showing response distributions and aggregate scores for each Activity in the Lesson.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Report breaks down results for each Activity; an Activity appears in many Reports (one per delivery).
relationships.sorts: By position in lesson (default), by average score, by response count.
relationships.filters: By activity type, by scoring method, by score range.
relationships.dependencies: Activity breakdowns reference the Lesson's activity configuration at the time of delivery.

nestedObjects.name: Activity
nestedObjects.description: Per-activity breakdown data within the report

nestedObjects.name: Student
nestedObjects.description: Individual student results within the report

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: Individual scores aggregated in the report

allAttributes.name: Report ID
allAttributes.description: Unique system identifier for the report.

allAttributes.name: Report Type
allAttributes.description: Type of delivery covered. Values: live-session-report, self-paced-report, combined-report.

allAttributes.name: Session/Assignment Reference
allAttributes.description: Reference to the specific delivery instance (Live Session ID or Assignment ID).

allAttributes.name: Class
allAttributes.description: The class that participated in the delivery.

allAttributes.name: Teacher
allAttributes.description: The teacher who conducted the delivery.

allAttributes.name: Student Count
allAttributes.description: Number of students with data in this report.

allAttributes.name: Participation Rate
allAttributes.description: Percentage of enrolled students who submitted at least one response.

allAttributes.name: Average Score
allAttributes.description: Mean score percentage across all students and scored activities.

allAttributes.name: Activity Breakdown
allAttributes.description: Per-activity results including response counts, averages, and distributions.

allAttributes.name: Export Format
allAttributes.description: Download format when exporting. Values: pdf, csv.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Report lifecycle state. Values: processing, available, archived.

allAttributes.name: Time Period
allAttributes.description: Date range covered by the report (e.g., session start to end, assignment window).

structure.evidence: A Report has comprehensive attributes: ID, type, lesson/session references, class, teacher, generated date, student count, participation rate, average score, activity breakdowns, and export options. It supports a full analytics detail page.

instances.evidence: Report for 'Intro to Ecosystems' (live, 3rd Grade-A, 92% participation, 78% avg), Report for 'Fractions' (self-paced, 5th Grade-Math, 85% participation, 65% avg). Every lesson delivery generates a report.

purpose.evidence: Reports close the teaching loop. Teachers use them to assess understanding, identify struggling students, inform reteaching decisions, and communicate results to stakeholders. They are the primary analytics output of every Nearpod delivery.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain object serving as the analytics output of the Nearpod delivery cycle. The feedback mechanism that connects instruction to outcomes.

synonyms.notes: Most common informal reference after a session
Used when sharing with non-teacher audiences

objectViews.description: Available reports shown as summary cards with participation rates, scores, and dates. Teachers browse recent reports and open the ones they need.
The complete report — participation, scores, activity breakdowns, and student-level detail. Teachers analyze results, export data, share with colleagues, or compare across periods.
Reports listed as compact rows with key metrics for batch selection and export. Teachers pick the reports they need and download the data.
